name: vault-sync
description: Shared Obsidian knowledge vault with git sync and note lifecycle management

skills.name: setup
skills.description: Set up the shared knowledge vault: clone vault repo, configure MCP server, install hooks

skills.name: promote
skills.description: Review and promote exploratory notes to established status after branch merge

skills.name: cleanup
skills.description: Find and remove exploratory notes for abandoned/deleted branches

skills.name: vault-note
skills.description: Create a new note in the vault with proper frontmatter and wikilinks

hooks.name: pre-write-pull
hooks.prompt: Before executing this vault write, call the vault-pull MCP tool to sync latest changes. If vault-pull returns a conflict error, BLOCK this tool call and report the conflict to the user.

hooks.name: post-write-push
hooks.prompt: After this vault write completes, call the vault-push MCP tool to commit and push changes. If push fails, warn the user but do not roll back.

hooks.name: session-start-check
hooks.prompt: Check if the current project branch is main. If so, call vault-query with status=exploratory to find notes from recently merged branches. If any exist, tell the user: 'Found N exploratory notes from recently merged branches. Run /promote-to-vault to review.'

hooks.name: observation-suggestion
hooks.prompt: Review what you just wrote. If it involved an architectural decision, a convention discovery, or a notable debugging solution, suggest saving it to the vault. Say: 'This looks like it could be worth saving to the vault: <suggested claim title>. Save it? (y/n)'. Only suggest at most once per user prompt — do not ask multiple times in a single response.

agents.name: promoter
agents.description: Reviews exploratory notes from a merged branch and proposes which to promote, update, or discard
